mup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 10)\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(current_step: int) -> float:\\n        if current_step < warmup_steps:\\n            return float(current_step + 1) / float(warmup_steps)\\n        progress = (current_step - warmup_steps) / float(max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps))\\n        return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"Under a fixed 256-step budget, introducing a schedule tied directly to total training steps should improve optimization efficiency versus a flat LR; adding short warmup plus cosine decay is a compact, robust default. A modest LR increase to 1e-3 complements this schedule and often improves early progress in small-budget CNN training.\",\"secondary_modes\":[\"topk\"]}"}}
